reasoning: The baseline rate of 'Explosions/Remote violence' in Kenya is around 2.25 events per 30 days. To trigger a YES resolution, the count in a single 30-day period must exceed 32.5 (i.e. at least 33 events). This represents a roughly 15-fold increase over the average and would require an intense conventional war, a massive wave of terror bombings, or a major airstrike campaign. A comprehensive search of recent news covering Kenya in January and early February 2026 reveals only isolated IED incidents (e.g. 1-2 events), completely consistent with the historical baseline of Al-Shabaab border attacks. There are no ongoing military campaigns or sudden spikes in remote violence. Given the sheer scale of the required localized violence increase and the absence of any indications of such an escalation, the probability of hitting >32.5 events in either 30-day window is virtually zero. I assigned the minimum allowable probability (0.02) for the near-term resolution date, and marginally higher (0.03) for the slightly more distant date to reflect time horizon uncertainty.
